dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8910 IH: Stop Letting United States Heads Funnel Unauthorized Nontransparent Dollars Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Thompson of California (for himself, Mr. Doggett, Mr. Larson of Connecticut, Mr. Davis of Illinois, Ms. Sánchez, Ms. Sewell, Ms. DelBene, Ms. Chu, Ms. Moore of Wisconsin, Mr. Boyle of Pennsylvania, Mr. Beyer, Mr. Evans of Pennsylvania, Mr. Schneider, Mr. Panetta, Mr. Gomez, Mr. Horsford, and Mr. Suozzi) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose a tax on specified settlement fund payments, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Stop Letting United States Heads Funnel Unauthorized Nontransparent Dollars Act of 2026 or the SLUSH FUND Act of 2026.

section.header: Imposition of tax on specified settlement fund payments

subsection.text: Subtitle D of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new chapter:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 5000E. Imposition of tax on specified settlement fund payments.

section.header: Imposition of tax on specified settlement fund payments

subsection.text: There is hereby imposed on any taxpayer for any taxable year a tax equal to 100 percent of any specified settlement fund payment received by such taxpayer during such taxable year.

paragraph.text: The term specified settlement fund payment means, with respect to any taxpayer for any taxable year, any amount received by such taxpayer during such taxable year from any fund, trust, or account the assets of which are derived from the outcome (whether by settlement, verdict, or otherwise) of any civil action which was filed by a specified person against the United States (or any agency or instrumentality thereof).

clause.text: any individual who has served as President of the United States,
any member of the family of such individual, and
any person controlled (based on principles similar to the principles which apply for purposes of section 52(b)) by one or more individuals described in clause (i) or (ii).

subparagraph.text: The term member of the family means, with respect to any individual described in subparagraph (A)(i)—

clause.text: the spouse of such individual, and
any individual who bears a relationship to such individual which is described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of section 152(d)(2).

paragraph.text: For purposes of subtitle F, any tax imposed by this section shall be treated as a tax imposed by subtitle A.
For purposes of chapter 1, the gross income of any taxpayer for any taxable year shall not include any specified settlement fund payment received by such taxpayer during such taxable year.

subsection.text: Section 275(a)(6) of such Code is amended by inserting 50B, after 50A,.

subsection.header: Failure To pay tax on specified settlement fund payments
subsection.text: Part I of subchapter A of chapter 68 of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new section:

section.header: Failure to pay tax on specified settlement fund payments
section.text: Any taxpayer who, with respect to any taxable year—

paragraph.text: willfully fails to pay the tax imposed by section 5000E(a), or
willfully attempts in any manner to evade or defeat such tax or the payment thereof,

section.continuation-text: shall, in addition to other penalties provided by law, be liable for a penalty of 50 percent of such tax for such taxable year.

paragraph.text: The table of chapters for subtitle D of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new item:
The table of sections for part I of subchapter A of chapter 68 of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 6660.Failure to pay tax on specified settlement fund payments.

paragraph.text: Except as provided by paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section shall apply with respect to amounts received on or after May 20, 2026.

paragraph.header: Failure to pay tax on specified settlement fund payments
paragraph.text: The amendment made by subsection (c) shall apply with respect to taxable years ending on or after May 20, 2026.

section.header: Returns relating to specified settlement fund payments

subsection.text: Subpart B of part III of subchapter A of chapter 61 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new section:

section.header: Returns relating to specified settlement fund payments

subsection.text: Every trustee, administrator, or other fiduciary who makes any specified settlement fund payment (as defined in section 5000E(b)) to any taxpayer during any taxable year shall make a return, according to the forms and regulations prescribed by the Secretary, setting forth—

paragraph.text: the aggregate amount of such payments received by such taxpayer during such taxable year, and
the name and address of such taxpayer.

subsection.header: Statements To be furnished with respect to whom information is required
subsection.text: Every person required to make a return under subsection (a) shall furnish to each taxpayer whose name is required to be set forth in such return a written statement—

paragraph.text: showing the identity of the trustee, administrator, or other fiduciary making the specified settlement fund payment,
showing the aggregate amount of such payments received by such taxpayer required to be shown on the return, and
notifying that such payments are subject to the tax imposed by section 5000E(a).

subsection.continuation-text: The written statement required under the preceding sentence shall be furnished to the taxpayer on or before January 31 of the year following the taxable year for which the return under subsection (a) was required to be made.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall, not later than 1 month following receipt of a return under subsection (a), make such return publicly available (in such form and manner as the Secretary determines appropriate).

subsection.header: Failure To file return with respect to specified settlement fund payments
subsection.text: Section 6652 of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.header: Failure To file return with respect to specified settlement fund payments
subsection.text: In the case of any failure to make a return required under section 6050BB which contains the information required by such section on the date prescribed therefor, unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause, there shall be paid (on notice and demand by the Secretary and in the same manner as tax) by the person failing to file such return, an amount equal to $10,000 for each such failure.
The table of sections for subpart B of part III of subchapter A of chapter 61 of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 6050BB.Returns relating to specified settlement fund payments.

paragraph.text: Except as provided by paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section shall apply with respect to amounts paid on or after May 20, 2026.

paragraph.header: Failure to file return with respect to specified settlement fund payments
paragraph.text: The amendment made by subsection (b) shall apply with respect to taxable years ending on or after May 20, 2026.
